fields.definition_origin: Server-Driven UI (SDUI) as a mobile architecture pattern crystallized at Airbnb (2017, 'A Deep Dive into Airbnb's Server-Driven UI System' — Airbnb Engineering blog), generalized via Lyft Plex (2019), Meta Litho (2018-2022), Spotify Hubs Framework (2017-2024), and DoorDash Mosaic (2021). Recent industry codification: 'Inside Lovi's Server-Driven Navigation' by Nikita Ortayev (medium.com/@ortayevn 2024), 'How to build the most scalable onboarding flow' by Lumen R&D (medium.com/lumen-rnd, 2024), Adapty Onboarding Builder release (2026) and Plotline's commercial SDUI onboarding product (2023-2026). The PDF 'Strategic Architecture and Psychological Frameworks for Mobile Application Onboarding' references SDUI as the highest-leverage iteration speedup for high-CAC apps.
fields.core_mechanism: SDUI inverts the traditional client-side rendering pipeline: instead of the client app containing hardcoded screen sequences, copy, and config, the server returns a JSON payload describing what to render (component tree + data) and the client is a generic interpreter that composes pre-built native components from the payload. This decouples onboarding iteration from App Store / Play Store review cycles (typically 1-7 days for review + ~30% of users on each release for ~2-4 weeks until full adoption). With SDUI, the team can ship a new onboarding variant by deploying a server config — instant rollout. Three load-bearing benefits: (1) experiment velocity (top performers run 14.7 paywall experiments/yr per Adapty 2026; SDUI is the enabling architecture); (2) market-specific variants (locale-routed payloads for JP vs US without binary fork); (3) instant kill-switch for regressions (item 45). Trade-offs: bundle complexity (interpreter + schema + fallback), latency on cold launch (must fetch config), backwards-compat burden (older app versions must still understand server payloads — schema versioning required), debugging complexity (config bug ≠ code bug, harder to grep). Critical when CAC is high — every day of slow iteration burns acquisition budget.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Airbnb engineering blog (2017, 2021 updates) — internal SDUI cut release cycle for search/listing flows from weeks to hours; cited as foundational architecture. (2) Lyft Plex (2019 talk + blog) — onboarding A/B test cycle reduced from weeks to <1 day. (3) Meta's React Native + Litho heavily SDUI-driven across Facebook/Instagram product surfaces. (4) Spotify Hubs (Tomáš Roose, public conference talks 2018-2022) — generic 'shelf' framework powers home, search, and onboarding from server JSON. (5) Adapty Onboarding Builder (2026 release): commercial SDUI onboarding tool, customer case studies cite 5-10x faster iteration vs in-binary onboarding. (6) Plotline (commercial RN-targeted SDUI 2023-2026) — customer case studies cite >50% reduction in time-to-launch for new flows. (7) Lovi's published onboarding flow architecture (Ortayev 2024) describes a custom protocol-oriented SDUI navigation system. (8) Mobbin teardowns — apps known to ship multiple onboarding variants per week (Cal AI, Headway, Headspace, Noom, BetterMe) almost certainly use SDUI internally though specifics not public. (9) RevenueCat 'State of Subscription Apps 2026' notes top-decile experimentation velocity correlates with SDUI architecture. Empirical evidence is largely industry case-study + vendor benchmarks; no peer-reviewed RCT.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Time-from-decision-to-rollout (target: <1 day for SDUI vs ~2 weeks for binary release), onboarding-experiments-per-quarter (RevenueCat/Adapty 2026 benchmark: top performers 14.7/yr), config-rollback time-to-revert (kill-switch latency), market-localized variants count, % of onboarding traffic on N>2 variants simultaneously.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (architectural pattern; no peer-reviewed RCT)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Indirect — SDUI itself is value-neutral; the EXPERIMENTS it enables drive conversion. Industry: 14.7 paywall experiments/yr (top performers, RevenueCat 2026) vs ~2-3/yr for non-SDUI teams; cumulative conversion lift over a year typically 20-50% per Adapty case studies (vendor-cited).
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Airbnb 2017+ search/listing/booking flows server-driven. (2) Lyft Plex (2019) ride-request and onboarding flows. (3) Meta Litho (2018+) — Facebook feed, profile, navigation server-driven. (4) Spotify Hubs Framework — home tab, search, daily mixes — same shelves rendered from JSON. (5) DoorDash Mosaic — order flow + onboarding server-driven. (6) Adapty Onboarding Builder (2026) — commercial: drag-and-drop authoring → JSON config → SDK rendering on iOS/Android/RN. (7) Plotline (2023-2026) — RN-focused commercial SDUI; named customers include several wellness apps. (8) Lovi (Ortayev 2024) — custom protocol-oriented navigation SDUI. (9) Cal AI's 33-step flow is rumored SDUI internally based on the velocity of variant rollouts (Mobbin captures show changes weekly). (10) Headspace 2024 onboarding A/B test cadence implies SDUI. (11) Noom's funnel iteration (multiple variants live simultaneously) is SDUI-grade. (12) Counter-example: Linear keeps onboarding in-binary because flows are stable and value of iteration < cost of architecture.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile has higher SDUI ROI because App Store / Play Store review cycle (1-7 days) creates the iteration penalty SDUI removes. Web releases continuously; SDUI on web is less load-bearing but still useful for AB testing and locale variants (Vercel/Next.js feature flags + edge config achieve similar benefits). Mobile-specific: must handle offline cold-start (cache last-known-good config), background termination (resume from server-stored state — item 33), versioning across staggered app updates. Web: same JSON payload pattern, simpler caching (CDN edge config).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: N/A architectural — the rendered UI uses platform-native components (Liquid Glass on iOS, M3E on Android), SDUI just composes them.
fields.category_fit: Strongest for: high-CAC apps (every iteration day burns budget), apps shipping multiple onboarding variants concurrently (locale × archetype × experiment), wellness/AI-wrapper categories with rapid funnel optimization. Acceptable for: mid-volume apps where 5-10 experiments/yr would be valuable. Weak fit: low-volume apps where 1-2 onboarding variants/yr suffices, very simple flows (<5 screens), apps where deep native integrations dominate (gaming with custom shaders, video editors). DO NOT use SDUI for safety-critical or regulated flows (KYC, medical intake) where binary auditability matters more than iteration speed.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_month for hand-rolled (build component catalog + schema + renderer + auth + caching). 1_week if using commercial SDK (Plotline, Adapty Onboarding Builder) — schema authoring + integration. Solo devs typically should use commercial SDK first, hand-roll later if cost or customization demands.
fields.tooling_options: COMMERCIAL: Adapty Onboarding Builder (2026, $99-$499/mo tiers, integrated paywalls), Plotline (RN-focused, custom pricing), Appcues Mobile, Pendo Mobile, Product Fruits. OPEN-SOURCE: OnboardJS (state machine focus, less SDUI), custom XState v5 + Zod schema. EXPERIMENTATION ROUTERS: PostHog (free tier), Statsig (generous free tier), Eppo, LaunchDarkly, ConfigCat. CDN/CONFIG SERVING: AWS AppConfig, GCP Remote Config (Firebase), Edge Config (Vercel), CloudFront with versioned JSON. SCHEMA VALIDATION: Zod (TS), ajv (JSON Schema). VISUAL EDITORS: Stately Studio (FSM), Adapty Builder (full SDUI), Plotline Builder.
fields.ai_dependency: none for SDUI itself. Optional: AI generation of variant copy / illustration / config can layer on top via internal tooling.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to define schema FIRST, renderer SECOND, components THIRD. Prompt fragment 1: 'Build a server-driven onboarding system for React Native. Define a Zod schema for the onboarding payload (version, flow_id, locale, archetype, theme_tokens, steps[], transitions). Each step has discriminated kind: hero | single_select | multi_select | numeric_input | theatrical_loader | reveal | paywall_side_by_side. Define a TypeScript type via z.infer. Build <OnboardingRenderer payload={config} /> that switches on step.kind and renders pre-built components (HeroScreen, SingleSelectScreen, etc.). FORBID: hardcoded screen sequences. FORBID: unknown step.kind crashing — provide fallback. Validate payload via Zod parse — invalid payloads route to bundled default config. Include schema version handling and 3-version backward compatibility.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Implement config fetching: on app launch, fetch /api/onboarding/config?user_id=X&locale=Y in parallel with splash; timeout 800ms; on timeout or error use cached last-known-good config from MMKV; on success cache to MMKV. Wire experiment_variant routing — server returns user-specific variant. Telemetry: emit onboarding_config_fetched { config_version, experiment_variant }, onboarding_config_fallback_used.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Output the example payload as a JSON file the team can iterate on without redeploying app binary. Include 8 step kinds covering full onboarding lifecycle (welcome, single-select, multi-select, numeric, theatrical_loader, reveal, side-by-side paywall, soft-paywall).'
fields.stitch_application: SDUI is non-visual; Stitch instructions focus on the rendered output. Prompt fragment: 'Mockup three onboarding screens that all render from the same component-catalog: SingleSelectScreen rendered with three different payload variants (variant A: 4 options with goal-icons, variant B: 3 options with photo-illustration, variant C: 4 options with letter-numbered hierarchy). Show that the visual differs only in payload props (theme_tokens, options array, illustration_style) — the component is identical. This proves the SDUI catalog approach.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to hardcoded screen-by-screen flow with copy in JSX strings. AI builds <Step1Screen />, <Step2Screen />, <Step3Screen /> as separate files with inline content. AI doesn't separate config from components. AI hardcodes routing in navigation. Counter-prompt: 'FORBID hardcoded screens — build a generic <OnboardingRenderer> that takes a payload prop. FORBID inline copy strings in components — all copy must come from props. FORBID per-step component files — single component-per-kind file (HeroScreen.tsx, SingleSelectScreen.tsx, etc., where kind is the discriminated step.kind). Output the Zod schema first, then the renderer, then the kind components, then a sample JSON payload. Wire telemetry that includes config_version and step_id.' AI sometimes returns to hardcoded for 'simplicity' — counter: 'maintaining decoupling is required even if it adds 30% more code; SDUI's value is iteration speed, not initial implementation speed.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) SDUI without schema validation — invalid configs crash production. (2) Schema versioning ignored — new fields break old app versions in field. (3) No offline fallback — failed config fetch means white screen on cold launch. (4) Component catalog under-built — every new onboarding idea requires new component (defeats purpose). (5) Over-flexible schema — payload can describe arbitrary layouts; engineering can never lock down the catalog. Strict kinds (hero, single_select, multi_select, numeric, loader, reveal, paywall) with closed enum. (6) Mixing SDUI with hardcoded sections — half-decoupled is worst-of-both. (7) Schema changes without designer involvement — config drift from product spec. (8) Performance: unbounded payload size, unbounded steps array. Cap payload <100KB, steps <50. (9) Security: payload XSS injection via stitched user content not sanitized. (10) Auditability: regulated flows (KYC) implemented via SDUI become harder to audit; keep regulated flows in binary.
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. SDUI's flexibility is a regulatory double-edge. POSITIVE: locale-routed compliance config (GDPR consent in EU, CCPA notice in CA) without binary release simplifies compliance. NEGATIVE: regulated flows (KYC, age gating, medical intake) implemented via SDUI complicate audit (auditor must inspect server config + payload history, not just binary). EU Digital Fairness Act draft Q4 2026: dark-pattern detection — SDUI ease of A/B testing manipulative variants is a risk vector; document experimentation governance. App Store / Play Store review: any onboarding shown to users must comply at runtime regardless of architecture; reviewer tests binary, not config — risk of shipping non-compliant config post-review. Mitigation: client-side compliance gates (age verification, consent UI) baked into the renderer, not the payload.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 4.7 (HTML5 / dynamic content) and 4.2.2 (Minimum Functionality) governance: SDUI permitted but Apple requires the app to perform meaningful function from the binary (not be a thin wrapper around server-driven content). Onboarding is bounded enough to not trigger 4.7. Google Play Developer Distribution Agreement permits SDUI freely. Apple Guideline 3.1.1 (IAP): IAP-related screens (paywall) MUST use Apple's IAP system — SDUI can describe layout but pricing/SKUs come from StoreKit, not server. Apple Guideline 3.1.2 toggle-paywall ban (2026-01) — SDUI payload schema must NOT support toggle-paywall variant; bake the side-by-side / timeline patterns into kind enums.
fields.accessibility_implications: SDUI must preserve a11y in component catalog: every kind component implements proper accessibilityRole, accessibilityLabel from props, focus management. Risk: new content via payload bypasses i18n a11y review — server-side authoring tool must include a11y validation (alt text required for images, label required for buttons). Dynamic Type / text scaling: kind components must flex with content (avoid fixed heights from payload). prefers-reduced-motion: kind components respect, payload may opt to disable specific motion via theme_tokens.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) SDUI is a 1-2 person-quarter investment — don't start until catalog need >5-10 variants. (2) Cold-start latency — config fetch must be parallel with splash, 800ms budget, fallback ready. (3) Versioning rigor — every payload field is forever (additive only); document schema in code review. (4) Authoring tool quality determines team velocity — bad authoring tool = SDUI = slower than hardcoded. (5) Debugging across binary version + config version + experiment variant is complex; PostHog session replay or UXCam essential. (6) Server outage strands users on outdated cached config — versioning + graceful degradation matters. (7) Schema bloat — every variant added widens schema; refactor periodically. (8) FSM (item 30) + SDUI integration: payload describes states + transitions, machine constructed at runtime; test machine validity on payload load. (9) Over-experimentation: 14.7 tests/yr is the top-decile; running 14 simultaneous tests dilutes signal — sequence experiments.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: SDUI is the canonical mechanism for serving culturally adapted onboarding without binary fork. JP variant (denser layouts, more social proof, formal copy register) coexists with US variant (sparser layouts, individualist copy) via locale-routed payload. RTL languages: payload can include direction flag; component catalog implements mirrored layouts. Scripts requiring different fonts (Hebrew, Arabic, CJK): theme_tokens.font_heading per locale. CN: payload can include WeChat-aware patterns (mini-program embed, social-proof testimonial style) absent in Western variants. Adapty's localization-test 62.3% LTV win rate (item 36) is operationally enabled by SDUI.
fields.prescribed_action: Build a strict, versioned JSON schema for onboarding payloads with closed step.kind enum (hero, single_select, multi_select, numeric_input, theatrical_loader, reveal, paywall_side_by_side, soft_paywall). Build a generic <OnboardingRenderer payload={config} /> that maps kind → component from a pre-built catalog. Validate every payload via Zod; fall back to bundled default on failure. Fetch config in parallel with splash (800ms budget, MMKV cache). Wire experiment_variant routing server-side. Forbid hardcoded onboarding sequences in app binary post-launch. Use commercial SDK (Plotline / Adapty Builder) for solo dev / startup phase; hand-roll later if scale demands.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — questionnaire content (item 26 sequencing, item 27 types, item 28 conversational pattern) is expressed as payload steps with kind=single_select / multi_select / numeric_input. Authoring tool lets PMs reorder questions or change copy without app release.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — SDUI enables rapid A/B testing of AI plan reveal variants (item 12), theatrical loader copy variants (item 13), stitching templates (item 14) — each iteration gets to optimal Aha faster than binary-locked flows.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: N/A architectural — SDUI is the mechanism that DELIVERS archetype-conditioned variants (item 52) at runtime; theme_tokens + content per archetype.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'binary-locked onboarding iteration' (slow A/B test cycle, 14.7-test gap). Pairs with item 30 (FSM) — payload describes states + transitions consumed by FSM. Pairs with item 32 (Persistence) — config_version stored alongside state. Pairs with item 45 (Feature Flags + A/B Test Infrastructure) — experiment routing layer drives which payload to serve.
sources: [Lumen R&D — Building the Most Scalable Onboarding Flow](https://medium.com/lumen-rnd/heres-how-to-build-the-most-scalable-onboarding-flow-e25ac5ad3371)
[Airbnb Engineering — A Deep Dive Into Airbnb's Server-Driven UI System](https://medium.com/airbnb-engineering/a-deep-dive-into-airbnbs-server-driven-ui-system-842244c5f5)
uncertain: specific aggregate conversion lift attributable purely to SDUI architecture (most evidence is iteration-velocity proxy)
